candidates.title: v0.6.6 release prep checker skeleton
candidates.source: Following the pattern of check_v065_release_prep.py, prepare a skeleton that validates absence of v0.6.6 artifacts while source version remains 0.6.5.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Planning mode rejects docs/releases/v0.6.6.md and 0.6.6 version bump; release-prep mode will accept them after bump.
candidates.reason: Standard pattern; prepares the release-prep gate without enabling it prematurely. No runtime changes.

candidates.title: Update checks-reference.md to reflect v0.6.5 released state
candidates.source: docs/development/checks-reference.md still frames v0.6.5 release prep checks as the current prep state.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: v0.6.5 release prep checks are described as historical/verified; the doc accurately reflects that v0.6.5 is released and the next planning line is v0.6.6.
candidates.reason: Low-cost docs hygiene that prevents maintainer confusion. No runtime changes.

candidates.title: Expand public docs consistency checker to validate release note references
candidates.source: scripts/check_public_docs_consistency.py could be expanded to validate that all docs/releases/v*.md files are referenced from CHANGELOG.md and that no stale Current Status (vX.Y.Z) strings remain in README.md.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Checker validates README status line matches current public release; warns on orphaned release notes not referenced in CHANGELOG.
candidates.reason: Low-cost automation that prevents recurring post-release docs drift. No runtime changes.

candidates.title: Harden release-checklist automated version reference validation
candidates.source: docs/release-checklist.md contains version-specific examples that drift after each release.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Checker validates that release-checklist examples reference the current public tag and source version without stale historical values.
candidates.reason: Valuable but broader than a minimal patch; defer to a dedicated docs-checker batch.

candidates.title: Normalize docs/trust/v0.6.5-status.md historical wording
candidates.source: The v0.6.5 trust status still contains some imperative checklist items that could be rephrased as historical statements.
candidates.acceptance_criteria: Trust status uses past-tense/historical wording consistently; no imperative items directed at a future reviewer.
candidates.reason: Cosmetic; does not affect correctness or checker behavior. Low priority.

rejected.title: Live trading default changes
rejected.reason: Safety boundary. Never changed in a patch.

rejected.title: Broker execution enablement
rejected.reason: Safety boundary. Never changed in a patch.

rejected.title: Provider execution unlock
rejected.reason: Safety boundary. Never changed in a patch.

rejected.title: PyPI publish
rejected.reason: Not approved in this planning batch; requires separate process.

rejected.title: Automatic skill activation
rejected.reason: Skill candidates remain manually reviewed and offline.

rejected.title: Automatic learning execution
rejected.reason: Learning suggestions remain offline and manually reviewed.

safety_boundaries: Live trading remains disabled by default.
Live submit remains disabled by default.
Provider execution remains disabled by default.
Broker execution remains disabled by default.
Risk gates remain deterministic and unchanged.
Human approval remains required for live order flow.
Credentials are not loaded unless explicitly configured.
No provider APIs, broker APIs, or real webhooks are called from CI or local checks.
constraints: Version is not bumped to 0.6.6 in this batch
No tag, GitHub release, or PyPI publish yet
release_criteria: dev_check, ci_check, and release_check --quick pass locally
CI on main is green after the prep commit is pushed
Version is bumped to 0.6.6 in pyproject.toml and src/atlas_agent/__init__.py
scripts/check_v066_release_prep.py and matching tests are created
Owner approval is obtained for tag cutover
